description: Expanded curated health-relevant DNA variant annotations for the personal health plugin, merged with local genetic.health reference knowledge across pharmacogenomics, cardiometabolic risk, traits, carrier signals, and monitoring guidance.
_comment: Merged local-first annotations from the genetic.health reference repo. Keep educational tone; route medication and disease claims back to clinician review.

variants.title: MTHFR A1298C
variants.description: Variant in the methylenetetrahydrofolate reductase gene affecting folate metabolism. The 1298C variant (heterozygous or homozygous) reduces enzyme activity, impairing conversion of folic acid to its active form L-methylfolate.

alleleEffects.summary: No impact on folate metabolism
May benefit from methylfolate over folic acid
Recommend methylfolate, B2, B6, B12 support; discuss with doctor

variants.title: MTHFR C677T
variants.description: The most studied MTHFR variant. The T allele reduces enzyme activity dramatically. Homozygous TT individuals have ~70% reduced activity and may have elevated homocysteine levels, especially with low folate intake.

alleleEffects.summary: No dietary modification needed
Consider folate-rich diet or methylfolate
Elevated homocysteine risk; methylfolate recommended; consult doctor

monitoring: Check homocysteine, folate, and B12 if symptoms or family history fit

supplementRecommendations.rationale: Supports folate-dependent methylation when MTHFR activity is reduced
May support MTHFR enzyme efficiency in susceptible genotypes

variants.title: MTHFR C776G
variants.description: Another MTHFR variant affecting enzyme thermolability and folate metabolism.

alleleEffects.summary: No action needed
Monitor folate intake
Consider methylfolate; check homocysteine

variants.title: MTHFR 3 SNP haplotype (additive with C677T and A1298C)
variants.description: This variant in combination with rs1801133 and rs4686302 can compound folate metabolism issues when multiple variant alleles are present.

alleleEffects.summary: No additional impact
More likely to benefit from methylfolate; check homocysteine

variants.title: COMT Val158Met (rs4683 variant proxy)
variants.description: COMT (Catechol-O-methyltransferase) breaks down dopamine, estrogens, and stress hormones. The Met/Met genotype results in lower enzyme activity, meaning slower dopamine clearance. This affects pain sensitivity, stress response, and cognitive style.

alleleEffects.effect: High COMT activity — fast dopamine clearance
alleleEffects.summary: Higher pain tolerance, stable mood; may need more stimulant meds to work
Balanced dopamine metabolism

alleleEffects.effect: Low COMT activity — slow dopamine clearance
alleleEffects.summary: More sensitive to pain and stress; heightened emotional responsiveness; caffeine may affect more

variants.title: COMT Val158Met
variants.description: The key COMT variant determining how quickly dopamine is cleared from the prefrontal cortex. Val/Val = efficient, Met/Met = slow. Affects personality traits (warrior vs worrier), response to stimulant medications, and pain sensitivity.

alleleEffects.effect: High enzyme activity — fast dopamine breakdown
alleleEffects.summary: Better focus under stress; may need higher stimulant dose
Balanced

alleleEffects.effect: Low enzyme activity — slow dopamine breakdown
alleleEffects.summary: More sensitive to pain and stress; lower stimulant dose often sufficient

variants.title: VDR BsmI (rs1544410)
variants.description: Vitamin D receptor variant affecting how cells respond to vitamin D. The BsmI variant (AA) is associated with lower vitamin D receptor density, potentially requiring higher vitamin D intake for equivalent biological effect.

alleleEffects.summary: May need higher vitamin D intake; test levels more frequently
Standard vitamin D guidelines likely sufficient
Normal vitamin D metabolism

variants.title: VDR FokI
variants.description: FokI variant in the VDR gene affects the structure of the vitamin D receptor. The C allele produces a longer VDR protein with reduced transcriptional activity.

alleleEffects.summary: Standard vitamin D guidelines
May need 20-30% more vitamin D for equivalent effect

variants.title: FTO rs9939609
variants.description: The most well-studied obesity-associated variant. FTO (Fat Mass and Obesity-Associated) affects food intake, satiety signaling, and energy expenditure. The A allele is associated with increased food-seeking behavior and higher BMI on high-fat diets.

alleleEffects.summary: Standard weight management approach
Pay attention to satiety cues; higher protein diet may help

alleleEffects.effect: Significantly increased food intake, higher BMI tendency
alleleEffects.summary: Structured meal timing helps; higher protein improves satiety; avoid high-fat diets

monitoring: Use labs and symptom context before supplement escalation

variants.title: FTO rs5082
variants.description: Another FTO variant in linkage disequilibrium with rs9939609. Affects food reward signaling and energy intake.

alleleEffects.summary: Standard dietary approaches
High protein, structured meals, avoid restrictive diets

monitoring: Use labs and symptom context before supplement escalation

variants.title: ACTN3 R577X (alternative call)
variants.description: Same variant as rs1815739, sometimes reported as R577X nomenclature in clinical reports.

alleleEffects.summary: Sprint/power events suit your genetics
Mixed training works well
Distance events suit you; build power through consistent training

variants.title: PPARGC1A Gly482Ser
variants.description: PPARGC1A (Peroxisome proliferator-activated receptor gamma coactivator 1-alpha) is a master regulator of mitochondrial biogenesis. The Ser variant is associated with reduced endurance training response — carriers show less improvement in VO2max with aerobic training.

alleleEffects.summary: Strong VO2max gains from endurance training
May need more volume/time for same gains
Consistency matters more; longer training cycles needed

variants.title: ADRB2 Arg16Gly
variants.description: Beta-2 adrenergic receptor variant affecting how cells respond to adrenaline and to bronchodilators (like albuterol/salbutamol). Gly/Gly individuals have enhanced bronchodilator response.

alleleEffects.summary: Standard asthma management if applicable
Lower doses of bronchodilators may be effective
Discuss with doctor if asthmatic; GWAS suggests Gly allele improves endurance training adaptation

variants.title: APOE Cys130Arg (rs429358)
variants.description: APOE gene encodes apolipoprotein E, which transports cholesterol in the brain and periphery. Combined with rs7412, determines APOE isoform (E2/E3/E4). E4 is the most studied Alzheimer's risk allele. IMPORTANT: having E4 does not mean you will develop Alzheimer's — it means increased statistical risk.

alleleEffects.summary: Average Alzheimer's and cardiovascular risk

alleleEffects.effect: APOE E4 heterozygous — ~3x increased Alzheimer's risk
alleleEffects.summary: Brain-healthy lifestyle particularly important; discuss with doctor
Prioritize cardiovascular and cognitive health; discuss monitoring with doctor

monitoring: Track blood pressure, lipids, hs-CRP, and clotting history where relevant

variants.title: APOE Arg176Cys (rs7412)
variants.description: Combined with rs429358 to determine APOE isoform. The T allele defines E2. E2 carriers have lower cardiovascular disease risk but may have higher triglyceride levels.

alleleEffects.summary: See rs429358
Neutral
Lower CVD risk but watch triglycerides

monitoring: Track blood pressure, lipids, hs-CRP, and clotting history where relevant

variants.title: TCF7L2 IVS3C>T
variants.description: TCF7L2 is one of the strongest genetic risk factors for Type 2 Diabetes. The T allele impairs insulin secretion (not insulin sensitivity). Carriers have ~1.4x increased T2D risk per copy.

alleleEffects.summary: Standard diabetes prevention
Maintain healthy weight; low-glycemic diet helps specifically for T allele carriers
Prioritize metabolic health; low-carb/low-glycemic diet particularly beneficial; discuss with doctor

monitoring: Use labs and symptom context before supplement escalation

variants.title: KCNJ11 E23K
variants.description: KCNJ11 encodes a subunit of the ATP-sensitive potassium channel in pancreatic beta cells. The K allele (23K) reduces insulin secretion in response to glucose, increasing T2D risk.

alleleEffects.summary: Standard metabolic health
Weight management reduces the risk
Low-glycemic diet important; maintain healthy weight

monitoring: Track fasting glucose, A1c, and post-meal energy / appetite patterns over time

variants.title: CYP2C9*2 Arg144Cys
variants.description: CYP2C9 is involved in warfarin and NSAID metabolism. The *2 allele (Cys144) reduces enzyme activity by ~30%. Carriers require lower warfarin doses (reduced metabolism → higher blood levels).

alleleEffects.summary: Standard warfarin/NSAID dosing
If on warfarin, expect lower dose requirement; discuss pharmacogenomics with doctor

monitoring: Review anticoagulant dosing with clinician when applicable

variants.title: CYP2C9*3 Ile359Leu
variants.description: CYP2C9*3 is a more severe loss-of-function allele (~80% reduced activity). Carriers need significantly lower warfarin doses. Also affects NSAIDs and sulfonylureas.

alleleEffects.summary: Standard dosing
Dramatically lower warfarin dose needed; reduced NSAID metabolism — use with caution

monitoring: Review anticoagulant dosing with clinician when applicable

variants.title: CYP2C19*2
variants.description: CYP2C19*2 is a loss-of-function allele affecting metabolism of proton pump inhibitors (PPIs), clopidogrel (Plavix), and some antidepressants. Poor metabolizers (PMs) — homozygous *2/*2 — have reduced ability to activate clopidogrel.

alleleEffects.summary: Standard drug doses
Reduced activation of clopidogrel; may need alternative PPI
Clopidogrel likely ineffective; discuss alternatives; reduced PPI efficacy

monitoring: Review CYP2C19-sensitive medications before prescribing changes

variants.title: CYP2C19*17
variants.description: CYP2C19*17 is an ultra-rapid metabolizer allele (gain of function). Increases enzyme activity. Carriers may have reduced plasma levels of PPIs (less effective) but better clopidogrel activation.

alleleEffects.summary: PPIs may be less effective; clopidogrel more effectively activated

monitoring: Consider faster-than-expected drug clearance when reviewing dosing

variants.title: FUT2 rs602662 (secretor status proxy)
variants.description: Another FUT2 variant strongly correlated with secretor status. Secretors (GG) can be infected by norovirus; non-secretors (AA/AG) are resistant.

alleleEffects.summary: Standard precautions during outbreaks
Natural protection during GI outbreaks

monitoring: Check B12 / MMA periodically if intake or symptoms raise concern

supplementRecommendations.rationale: FUT2-related absorption patterns can coincide with lower B12 status

variants.title: SLC45A2 (MATP) — Skin/Hair Pigmentation
variants.description: SLC45A2 (MATP) marker imported from the genetic.health expanded_analysis reference. One of the strongest skin lightening alleles in European populations.

alleleEffects.summary: The G allele (Leu374) is nearly fixed in Europeans — associated with light skin pigmentation.
Intermediate skin pigmentation.
Ancestral allele — associated with darker skin pigmentation.

monitoring: Track UV exposure, skin checks, and sun sensitivity
variants.traitSummary: One of the strongest skin lightening alleles in European populations.

variants.title: SERPINA1 Pi*S carrier marker
variants.description: SERPINA1 Pi*S can contribute to alpha-1 antitrypsin deficiency carrier patterns and respiratory vulnerability, especially alongside smoking or additional A1AT variants.
monitoring: Avoid smoking and discuss lung/liver follow-up if symptoms or family history fit

alleleEffects.summary: No Pi*S carrier pattern detected at this marker.
Carrier pattern; prioritize smoking avoidance and respiratory awareness.
Potentially stronger alpha-1 antitrypsin deficiency relevance; discuss clinician follow-up.

variants.title: CLOCK Sleep / Circadian Response
variants.description: CLOCK marker imported from the genetic.health comprehensive_snp_database reference. Normal CLOCK gene - standard circadian timing

alleleEffects.summary: Normal CLOCK gene - standard circadian timing
CLOCK 3111C heterozygous - slight evening preference
CLOCK 3111C heterozygous - slight evening preference
CLOCK 3111C homozygous - evening chronotype, may have delayed sleep phase

monitoring: Track sleep quality, stimulant timing, and recovery response

variants.title: MC1R — Skin
variants.description: MC1R marker imported from the genetic.health comprehensive_snp_database reference. Normal MC1R - standard sun sensitivity

alleleEffects.summary: Normal MC1R - standard sun sensitivity
MC1R R151C carrier - increased sun sensitivity, freckling, skin cancer risk
MC1R R151C carrier - increased sun sensitivity, freckling, skin cancer risk
MC1R R151C homozygous - red hair phenotype, very high sun sensitivity

monitoring: Track UV exposure, skin checks, and sun sensitivity
variants.traitSummary: Normal MC1R - standard sun sensitivity

variants.title: MC1R — Skin
variants.description: MC1R marker imported from the genetic.health comprehensive_snp_database reference. Normal MC1R R160W

alleleEffects.summary: Normal MC1R R160W
MC1R R160W carrier - increased sun sensitivity
MC1R R160W carrier - increased sun sensitivity
MC1R R160W homozygous - high sun/skin cancer risk

monitoring: Track UV exposure, skin checks, and sun sensitivity
variants.traitSummary: Normal MC1R R160W

variants.title: HLA-DQA1 — Autoimmune
variants.description: HLA-DQA1 marker imported from the genetic.health comprehensive_snp_database reference. Lower celiac disease risk

alleleEffects.summary: Lower celiac disease risk
HLA-DQ2.5 carrier - increased celiac disease risk
HLA-DQ2.5 carrier - increased celiac disease risk
HLA-DQ2.5 homozygous - highest celiac disease risk

monitoring: If symptomatic, discuss celiac serologies or gluten-related evaluation with clinician

variants.title: ADORA2A — Caffeine Response
variants.description: ADORA2A marker imported from the genetic.health comprehensive_snp_database reference. Normal anxiety response to caffeine

alleleEffects.summary: Normal anxiety response to caffeine
Intermediate caffeine-anxiety response
Intermediate caffeine-anxiety response
Increased anxiety response to caffeine - consider lower doses or alternatives

monitoring: Track anxiety and sleep disruption after caffeine intake

variants.title: CYP2D6 — Drug Metabolism
variants.description: CYP2D6 marker imported from the genetic.health comprehensive_snp_database reference. Normal CYP2D6 - standard codeine/tramadol metabolism

alleleEffects.summary: Normal CYP2D6 - standard codeine/tramadol metabolism
Intermediate CYP2D6 - reduced opioid activation
Intermediate CYP2D6 - reduced opioid activation
Poor CYP2D6 (*4/*4) - codeine ineffective, tramadol reduced

variants.title: ADORA2A — Caffeine Response
variants.description: ADORA2A marker imported from the genetic.health comprehensive_snp_database reference. Lower caffeine sensitivity - less anxiety from caffeine

alleleEffects.summary: Lower caffeine sensitivity - less anxiety from caffeine
Normal caffeine sensitivity
Normal caffeine sensitivity
High caffeine sensitivity - more prone to caffeine anxiety and sleep disruption

variants.title: AGT — Cardiovascular
variants.description: AGT marker imported from the genetic.health comprehensive_snp_database reference. Normal angiotensinogen - standard blood pressure

alleleEffects.summary: Normal angiotensinogen - standard blood pressure
AGT M235T heterozygous - ~20% higher AGT, slightly elevated BP risk
AGT M235T heterozygous - ~20% higher AGT, slightly elevated BP risk
AGT T/T - ~40% higher AGT levels, elevated hypertension risk, salt restriction helpful

monitoring: Track blood pressure, lipids, hs-CRP, and clotting history where relevant

variants.title: ADA — Caffeine Response
variants.description: ADA marker imported from the genetic.health comprehensive_snp_database reference. Normal adenosine deaminase - standard sleep pressure

alleleEffects.summary: Normal adenosine deaminase - standard sleep pressure
ADA G22A heterozygous - slower adenosine clearance, deeper sleep need
ADA G22A heterozygous - slower adenosine clearance, deeper sleep need
ADA G22A homozygous - high sleep pressure, may need more sleep

variants.title: CACNA1C — Mood / Psychiatric
variants.description: CACNA1C marker imported from the genetic.health expanded_analysis reference. One of the strongest GWAS hits for mood disorders. Affects neuronal calcium signaling and plasticity.

alleleEffects.summary: Associated with increased risk of bipolar disorder, major depression, and schizophrenia. Affects calcium channel signaling in neurons.
One risk allele — intermediate effect.
Normal CACNA1C calcium channel function.

monitoring: Track mood, anxiety, sleep, and stimulant response longitudinally
variants.traitSummary: One of the strongest GWAS hits for mood disorders. Affects neuronal calcium signaling and plasticity.

variants.title: TRPM8 Migraine Susceptibility
variants.description: TRPM8 marker imported from the genetic.health expanded_analysis reference. TRPM8 is a cold-sensing ion channel. Its role in migraine may relate to trigeminovascular pain pathways.

alleleEffects.summary: Homozygous risk allele at TRPM8 (cold/menthol receptor). Increased migraine susceptibility.
Heterozygous — moderately increased migraine risk.
Protective genotype at this locus — lower migraine susceptibility.

monitoring: Track migraine or pain triggers, sleep stability, and hydration
variants.traitSummary: TRPM8 is a cold-sensing ion channel. Its role in migraine may relate to trigeminovascular pain pathways.

variants.title: TAS2R38 — Bitter Taste Sensitivity
variants.description: TAS2R38 marker imported from the genetic.health expanded_analysis reference. Third component of TAS2R38 bitter taste haplotype.

alleleEffects.summary: AVI haplotype component — non-tasting.
Heterozygous — intermediate bitter perception.
PAV haplotype component — taster.

variants.traitSummary: Third component of TAS2R38 bitter taste haplotype.

variants.title: TP53 — Longevity
variants.description: TP53 marker imported from the genetic.health comprehensive_snp_database reference. TP53 Pro72 - more efficient apoptosis, may be protective against cancer

alleleEffects.summary: TP53 Pro72 - more efficient apoptosis, may be protective against cancer
TP53 Pro72Arg heterozygous - balanced
TP53 Pro72Arg heterozygous - balanced
TP53 Arg72 - less efficient apoptosis

monitoring: Revisit recovery, metabolic health, and long-horizon prevention annually
variants.traitSummary: TP53 Pro72 - more efficient apoptosis, may be protective against cancer

variants.title: DRD2 — Dopamine System
variants.description: DRD2 marker imported from the genetic.health expanded_analysis reference. In linkage with rs2283265. Together they predict prefrontal dopamine signaling efficiency.

alleleEffects.summary: Normal D2L/D2S ratio.
Modified isoform ratio — may affect prefrontal cortex function.
Substantially altered — associated with impaired prefrontal cognition.

monitoring: Track mood, anxiety, sleep, and stimulant response longitudinally
variants.traitSummary: In linkage with rs2283265. Together they predict prefrontal dopamine signaling efficiency.

variants.title: TERC — Telomere Length / Aging
variants.description: TERC marker imported from the genetic.health expanded_analysis reference. TERC provides the RNA template for telomerase. Variants affect telomere maintenance.

alleleEffects.summary: Normal TERC (telomerase RNA component) — standard telomere maintenance.
One copy of shorter-telomere allele.
Associated with shorter telomere length and accelerated cellular aging.

monitoring: Revisit recovery, metabolic health, and long-horizon prevention annually
variants.traitSummary: TERC provides the RNA template for telomerase. Variants affect telomere maintenance.

variants.title: LRP1 Migraine Susceptibility
variants.description: LRP1 marker imported from the genetic.health expanded_analysis reference. LRP1 is involved in lipoprotein clearance and neuronal signaling. One of the top migraine GWAS loci.

alleleEffects.summary: Homozygous risk — increased migraine susceptibility.
Heterozygous — moderately increased risk.
Normal migraine risk at this locus.

monitoring: Track migraine or pain triggers, sleep stability, and hydration
variants.traitSummary: LRP1 is involved in lipoprotein clearance and neuronal signaling. One of the top migraine GWAS loci.

variants.title: GSTP1 — Detoxification
variants.description: GSTP1 marker imported from the genetic.health comprehensive_snp_database reference. Normal GSTP1 Ala114 function

alleleEffects.summary: Normal GSTP1 Ala114 function
GSTP1 Ala114Val heterozygous
GSTP1 Ala114Val heterozygous
GSTP1 Val/Val at 114 - further reduced activity

monitoring: Monitor recovery load, toxin exposures, and liver-related context when relevant

supplementRecommendations.rationale: Supports phase-II detoxification and antioxidant signaling

variants.title: HIF1A — Hypoxia Response / Aging
variants.description: HIF1A marker imported from the genetic.health expanded_analysis reference. HIF-1alpha is the master regulator of oxygen-sensing pathways. Enhanced stability may improve exercise adaptation and wound healing.

alleleEffects.summary: Normal hypoxia response.
Pro582Ser heterozygous — enhanced HIF-1alpha stability. Associated with better adaptation to low oxygen, potentially beneficial for endurance and tissue repair.
Pro582Ser homozygous — substantially enhanced hypoxia response.

monitoring: Revisit recovery, metabolic health, and long-horizon prevention annually
variants.traitSummary: HIF-1alpha is the master regulator of oxygen-sensing pathways. Enhanced stability may improve exercise adaptation and wound healing.

variants.title: IRF4 — Skin
variants.description: IRF4 marker imported from the genetic.health comprehensive_snp_database reference. Normal pigmentation regulation

alleleEffects.summary: Normal pigmentation regulation
IRF4 variant - tendency toward lighter skin, increased sun sensitivity
IRF4 variant - tendency toward lighter skin, increased sun sensitivity
IRF4 T/T - very light skin, high sun sensitivity, extra sun protection needed

monitoring: Track UV exposure, skin checks, and sun sensitivity
variants.traitSummary: Normal pigmentation regulation

variants.title: ADH1B — Alcohol
variants.description: ADH1B marker imported from the genetic.health comprehensive_snp_database reference. Slower alcohol metabolism - alcohol effects last longer

alleleEffects.summary: Slower alcohol metabolism - alcohol effects last longer
Intermediate alcohol metabolism
Intermediate alcohol metabolism
Fast alcohol metabolism - protective against alcoholism

variants.title: ARNTL Sleep / Circadian Response
variants.description: ARNTL marker imported from the genetic.health comprehensive_snp_database reference. Normal BMAL1 function

alleleEffects.summary: Normal BMAL1 function
BMAL1 variant - may affect circadian amplitude
BMAL1 variant - may affect circadian amplitude
BMAL1 variant homozygous - may have weaker circadian rhythm

monitoring: Track sleep quality, stimulant timing, and recovery response

variants.title: KITLG — Hair Color
variants.description: KITLG marker imported from the genetic.health expanded_analysis reference. Major determinant of blonde vs brown hair in Europeans.

alleleEffects.summary: Typical — dark hair (brown/black) expected.
One copy of blonde-associated allele.
Strongly associated with blonde hair in Europeans.

monitoring: Track UV exposure, skin checks, and sun sensitivity
variants.traitSummary: Major determinant of blonde vs brown hair in Europeans.

variants.title: SLC24A4 — Eye Color
variants.description: SLC24A4 marker imported from the genetic.health expanded_analysis reference. Secondary eye/hair color gene.

alleleEffects.summary: Associated with darker eye and hair color.
Intermediate effect on pigmentation.
Associated with lighter eye and hair color.

variants.traitSummary: Secondary eye/hair color gene.

variants.title: HERC2/OCA2 — Eye Color
variants.description: HERC2/OCA2 marker imported from the genetic.health expanded_analysis reference. This single SNP explains ~75% of eye color variation in Europeans.

alleleEffects.summary: Strongly predicts blue or green eye color. This is the strongest single predictor of light eyes in Europeans.
One copy of blue-eye allele. Green, hazel, or light brown eyes likely.
Strongly predicts brown eye color.

variants.traitSummary: This single SNP explains ~75% of eye color variation in Europeans.

variants.title: BCMO1 — Nutrition
variants.description: BCMO1 marker imported from the genetic.health comprehensive_snp_database reference. Normal beta-carotene conversion to vitamin A

alleleEffects.summary: Normal beta-carotene conversion to vitamin A
BCMO1 A379V heterozygous - ~30% reduced conversion, consider preformed vitamin A
BCMO1 A379V heterozygous - ~30% reduced conversion, consider preformed vitamin A
BCMO1 T/T - ~70% reduced beta-carotene conversion, need preformed vitamin A sources

monitoring: Use labs and symptom context before supplement escalation

variants.title: TYR — Eye Color / Freckling
variants.description: TYR marker imported from the genetic.health expanded_analysis reference. TYR is the master melanin enzyme. Variants affect eye color AND freckle tendency.

alleleEffects.summary: Standard TYR — darker eye color and fewer freckles expected.
Reduced tyrosinase activity — lighter eyes and increased freckling.
Substantially reduced tyrosinase — strongly associated with blue eyes and freckling.

monitoring: Track UV exposure, skin checks, and sun sensitivity
variants.traitSummary: TYR is the master melanin enzyme. Variants affect eye color AND freckle tendency.

GSTP1 Ile105Val heterozygous - reduced detox capacity

variants.title: KIBRA (WWC1) — Memory Performance
variants.description: KIBRA (WWC1) marker imported from the genetic.health expanded_analysis reference. KIBRA is involved in synaptic plasticity and memory consolidation in the hippocampus.

alleleEffects.summary: T/T associated with better episodic memory performance in multiple studies.
Heterozygous — still associated with better memory than CC.
Normal memory performance at this locus. This is the more common genotype.

monitoring: Track memory, focus, and sleep-dependent cognitive swings over time

variants.title: TAS2R38 — Bitter Taste Sensitivity
variants.description: TAS2R38 marker imported from the genetic.health expanded_analysis reference. Second component of TAS2R38 bitter taste haplotype.

alleleEffects.summary: AVI haplotype component — associated with non-tasting.
Heterozygous — intermediate bitter perception.
PAV haplotype component — associated with tasting.

variants.description: FADS1 marker imported from the genetic.health comprehensive_snp_database reference. FADS1 T/T - efficient omega-3/6 conversion, plant sources adequate

alleleEffects.summary: Wet, sticky earwax (dominant in European/African populations). Also associated with more body odor / apocrine gland activity.
Wet earwax (wet is dominant). Standard body odor.

variants.title: TPMT — Drug Metabolism

variants.description: ALDH2 marker imported from the genetic.health comprehensive_snp_database reference. Normal ALDH2 - efficient alcohol metabolism

alleleEffects.summary: Normal ALDH2 - efficient alcohol metabolism
ALDH2*2 heterozygous - alcohol flush, increased esophageal cancer risk with drinking
ALDH2*2 heterozygous - alcohol flush, increased esophageal cancer risk with drinking
ALDH2*2 homozygous - severe alcohol intolerance, avoid alcohol

variants.title: SCN9A — Pain Sensitivity
variants.description: SCN9A marker imported from the genetic.health expanded_analysis reference. SCN9A encodes Nav1.7, a sodium channel critical for pain signaling. Loss-of-function causes congenital insensitivity to pain; gain-of-function causes extreme pain disorders.

alleleEffects.summary: Normal sodium channel Nav1.7 function — standard pain perception.
One copy of variant — associated with lower pain threshold and increased pain sensitivity.
Homozygous — significantly increased pain sensitivity. SCN9A gain-of-function increases nociceptor excitability.

monitoring: Track migraine or pain triggers, sleep stability, and hydration
variants.traitSummary: SCN9A encodes Nav1.7, a sodium channel critical for pain signaling. Loss-of-function causes congenital insensitivity to pain; gain-of-function causes extreme pain disorders.

variants.title: TAS2R38 — Bitter Taste Sensitivity
variants.description: TAS2R38 marker imported from the genetic.health expanded_analysis reference. Part of TAS2R38 haplotype. Combine with rs1726866 and rs10246939 for full prediction.

alleleEffects.summary: Cannot taste PROP/PTC bitter compounds. Cruciferous vegetables (broccoli, Brussels sprouts) taste mild.
Intermediate bitter taste perception. Some sensitivity to bitter compounds.
Strong bitter taste perception. Broccoli, Brussels sprouts, dark chocolate, coffee may taste very bitter.

variants.traitSummary: Part of TAS2R38 haplotype. Combine with rs1726866 and rs10246939 for full prediction.

variants.title: COL5A1 — Fitness
variants.description: COL5A1 marker imported from the genetic.health comprehensive_snp_database reference. COL5A1 C/C - more flexible tendons, lower injury risk

alleleEffects.summary: COL5A1 C/C - more flexible tendons, lower injury risk
COL5A1 heterozygous - intermediate tendon properties
COL5A1 heterozygous - intermediate tendon properties
COL5A1 T/T - stiffer tendons, higher injury risk, more warm-up needed

variants.title: OR6A2 — Cilantro Taste
variants.description: OR6A2 marker imported from the genetic.health expanded_analysis reference. The soapy taste perception of cilantro is largely genetic, mediated by olfactory receptor OR6A2.

alleleEffects.summary: Standard OR6A2 — cilantro tastes herbal/citrusy.
One copy — slight tendency to perceive cilantro as soapy, though many carriers still enjoy it.
Strongly associated with perceiving cilantro as having a soapy, unpleasant taste.

variants.traitSummary: The soapy taste perception of cilantro is largely genetic, mediated by olfactory receptor OR6A2.

variants.title: STAT4 — Autoimmune
variants.description: STAT4 marker imported from the genetic.health comprehensive_snp_database reference. Normal autoimmune risk at this locus

alleleEffects.summary: Normal autoimmune risk at this locus
STAT4 risk allele - increased RA, lupus risk
STAT4 risk allele - increased RA, lupus risk
STAT4 T/T - elevated autoimmune disease risk

monitoring: Reassess symptoms and discuss targeted testing if compatible symptoms emerge

variants.title: CYP1A2 Caffeine Metabolism
variants.description: CYP1A2 marker imported from the genetic.health comprehensive_snp_database reference. Fast caffeine metabolizer - clears caffeine quickly, lower cardiovascular risk from coffee

alleleEffects.summary: Fast caffeine metabolizer - clears caffeine quickly, lower cardiovascular risk from coffee
Intermediate caffeine metabolizer - moderate clearance, ~5-6hr half-life
Slow caffeine metabolizer - caffeine lingers 8-12hrs, increased cardiovascular risk with high intake

monitoring: Track sleep quality after caffeine
Use an early-day caffeine cutoff if sleep is fragile

variants.title: TERT — Telomere Length / Aging
variants.description: TERT marker imported from the genetic.health expanded_analysis reference. Independent TERT variant affecting telomere biology.

alleleEffects.summary: Second TERT variant — associated with shorter telomere length.
Intermediate telomere length effect.
Associated with longer telomere length at this locus.

monitoring: Revisit recovery, metabolic health, and long-horizon prevention annually
variants.traitSummary: Independent TERT variant affecting telomere biology.

variants.title: CYP3A5 — Drug Metabolism
variants.description: CYP3A5 marker imported from the genetic.health comprehensive_snp_database reference. CYP3A5 expressor - may need higher tacrolimus doses

alleleEffects.summary: CYP3A5 expressor - may need higher tacrolimus doses
Intermediate CYP3A5 expression
Intermediate CYP3A5 expression
CYP3A5 non-expressor (*3/*3) - standard tacrolimus dosing

variants.title: PEMT — Methylation
variants.description: PEMT marker imported from the genetic.health comprehensive_snp_database reference. Normal PEMT - adequate choline synthesis

alleleEffects.summary: Normal PEMT - adequate choline synthesis
PEMT G5765A heterozygous - may need more dietary choline
PEMT G5765A heterozygous - may need more dietary choline
PEMT G5765A homozygous - higher choline requirements, especially for women

monitoring: Track choline intake and liver markers if intake is chronically low

supplementRecommendations.rationale: PEMT variants can raise dietary choline requirements

variants.title: ABO Blood Type Marker
variants.description: ABO marker imported from the genetic.health expanded_analysis reference. This deletion determines O vs non-O blood type.

alleleEffects.summary: Homozygous deletion — Blood Type O (no A or B antigens).
One functional copy + one O deletion. Blood type is A or B (depends on rs8176746).
Both copies functional. Blood type is A, B, or AB (depends on rs8176746).
Could not determine genotype.

variants.traitSummary: ABO deletion marker helps determine O versus non-O blood group patterns.

variants.title: ABO Blood Type Marker
variants.description: ABO marker imported from the genetic.health expanded_analysis reference. Distinguishes A from B blood type. Must combine with rs8176719.

alleleEffects.summary: Both copies encode A transferase. Combined with rs8176719, helps determine A vs O.
One A and one B allele — if both functional, blood type AB.
Both copies encode B transferase.

variants.traitSummary: ABO transferase marker helps distinguish A versus B antigen patterns.

variants.description: VKORC1 marker imported from the genetic.health comprehensive_snp_database reference. Normal warfarin sensitivity

alleleEffects.summary: Normal warfarin sensitivity
Increased warfarin sensitivity - lower doses needed
Increased warfarin sensitivity - lower doses needed
Highly warfarin sensitive - significantly lower doses
